title: Podman auto update doesn't use credentials from quadlet .image
body:
### Issue Description

I have a `service.container` with:
```
[Container]
Image=service.image
AutoUpdate=registry
...
```

and `service.image`:
```
[Image]
Image=ghcr.io/user/private-repo:latest
Creds=user:password
```

Podman auto updates don't work as it doesn't use the credentials.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create an image in a registry requiring credentials to pull
2. Create a quadlet .container -file referencing a .image -file with auto update from registry enabled
3. In the .image -file pointing to the image in the private registry with credentials
4. Start the container. This will pull the image and start it.
5. Run `podman-auto-update.service`. It will fail to pull the image as it doesn't use the credentials.


### Describe the results you received

When podman-auto-update.service runs, it fails with:
`Error: checking image updates for container <container hash>: unable to retrieve auth token: invalid username/password: unauthorized`

### Describe the results you expected

I expected it to use the same credentials originally used to pull the image

### podman info output

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
I am marking this as feature as it currently works as expected. podman auto-update is a separate command that has no idea about the existence of quadlet files.
Currently it is required to pass the authfile to the command or set the `io.containers.autoupdate.authfile` container label https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-auto-update.1.html#authfile-path

I think it would be a reasonable ask to make podman set the label automatically if AutoUpdate= is configured in the quadlet file.

cc @ygalblum

It seems setting the label won't be enough as the image service doesn't generate an auth file. So it would either need to be generated somewhere or auto-update would need to be capable of using the credentials directly, or maybe from a podman secret.

Or maybe the image service could set some label referring to itself, for example `io.containers.autoupdate.imageservice=service-image.service` and auto update could see that and just restart it to pull the new image. This would also ensure any other options specified in the .image file are honored.

I like the idea that Quadlet will add the label pointing to the Auth File if AutoUpdate is set. It can take the value either from the `AutoFile` key of the `.container` (currently isn't supported, but can be easily added) or the `.image` file if the image points to a `.image` file. But, as @uumas pointed, in the example in the description, there is no target auth file.

We can complicate it a bit more and add a new Quadlet unit to create auth files...

Ah I missed the Creds part. I don't think using the cli arg like this is great for security.
The command will execute `podman pull --creds user:password ...` which means your username will be leaked to anyone who can view the process list which by default is everyone.

quadlet cannot really (sanely) generate the authfile of this because the authfile is per registry so it would then to also parse the image name to get the registry domain to have the authfile. And then if we generate a tmp auth file we would need to have a place to store it consistently and remove it it again on service stop, etc...
Lots of small details to think about.

So I would say the fix should be teach quadlet to add the authfile label and encourage users to use Authfile= if they need that to work.

> quadlet cannot really (sanely) generate the autofile of this because the authfile is per registry so it would then to also parse the image name to get the registry domain to have the authfile.

I meant a new Quadlet unit file (e.g. `.authfile`). The unit file will take the registry URL, credentials (to avoid leaking, users can certs or a secret) and target location of the auth file as inputs and run `podman login`. Then, other units, e.g. `.container` or `.image`, can reference the `.authfile` in order to point to the file it generated.

So, basically the idea is to make a Quadlet unit for your last line: 
> So I would say the fix should be teach quadlet to add the authfile label and encourage users to use Authfile= if they need that to work.
Ah got it now, yes that does sound like a interesting idea I would be in favour of.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

The image unit credentials are apparently not being honored at all. Even starting the systemd service without prior login to the registry is not possible in our case. It is unclear to me why the Creds option is documented but not working as expected after reading https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-systemd.unit.5.html

Didn't try AuthFile yet.

@mendorf My idea for the `.login` file to be used in different files was not accepted. But, one reason was that it basically replaces the usage of an AuthFile. 
So, I guess the way to address this requirement is to generate the authfile once and use it for both the `.image` file (using the `AuthFile` key) and the `.container` file (using the proper label)
